meta.name: MHC-SF Mental Health Continuum
meta.description: The MHC-SF is a 14-item self-report measure of positive mental health assessing emotional, social, and psychological well-being.
meta.author: Keyes CLM (2005)
meta.reference: Keyes CLM. Mental illness and/or mental health? Investigating axioms of the complete state model of health. J Consult Clin Psychol. 2005;73(3):539-548.
meta.instruction: During the past month, how often did you feel...

questions.text: That you had something important to contribute to society
That you belonged to a community (like a social group, your school, or your neighborhood)
That our society is becoming a better place for people
That the way our society works makes sense to you
That you liked most parts of your personality
Good at managing the responsibilities of your daily life
That you had warm and trusting relationships with others
That you had experiences that challenged you to grow and become a better person
Confident to think or express your own ideas and opinions
That your life has a sense of direction or meaning to it

ranges.label: Languishing
ranges.description: Your positive mental health is low. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Focus on positive experiences
Build meaningful social connections
Consider consulting a professional
Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging

ranges.label: Moderate Mental Health
ranges.description: Your positive mental health is moderate. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Review subscale scores
Increase positive social activities
Practice gratitude and mindfulness
Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle
Share your experiences and positive feelings with others

ranges.label: Flourishing
ranges.description: Your positive mental health is high. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle
Share positive energy with others
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
Maintain gratitude and mindfulness practices

emotional.label: Low
emotional.description: Emotional well-being is low. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

emotional.label: Moderate
emotional.description: Emotional well-being is moderate. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

emotional.label: High
emotional.description: Emotional well-being is high. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness

social.label: Low
social.description: Social well-being is low. Your social concerns are notable and may be significantly affecting your relationships and quality of life. Professional support is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Professional treatment for social anxiety or loneliness can be very effective
Start practicing gradually in safe social environments
Ensure basic interpersonal connections and support
Develop a social improvement plan under professional guidance

social.label: Moderate
social.description: Social well-being is moderate. At this level, social difficulties may cause feelings of isolation or discomfort in interpersonal interactions, affecting life satisfaction.
suggestions: Consider discussing social concerns with a counselor
Join social skills training or support groups
Gradually challenge social avoidance behaviors
Develop one or two deeper friendships
Focus on building self-worth

social.label: High
social.description: Social well-being is high. This indicates that your social functioning and relationships are in good condition, maintaining satisfactory social connections.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current social relationships
Keep an open and communicative attitude
Participate in social activities that interest you
Focus on the quality rather than quantity of relationships
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness

psychological.label: Low
psychological.description: Psychological well-being is low. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging
Explore new interests and activities
Practice a gratitude journal, focusing on positive aspects of life
If you consistently feel unmotivated, consider consulting a professional

psychological.label: Moderate
psychological.description: Psychological well-being is moderate. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle
Share your experiences and positive feelings with others
Helping others is also an effective way to enhance well-being
Continue developing personal interests and social networks

psychological.label: High
psychological.description: Psychological well-being is high. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
Maintain gratitude and mindfulness practices
Focus on continued growth and deepening life meaning
